todaysEvents.summary: Houthis fired first ballistic missile at Israel from Yemen on Mar 28 - intercepted by Israeli air defenses; first Houthi strike on Israel since the Iran war began Feb 28; marks Iran-backed multi-front expansion into a second theatre (CNBC, Al Jazeera, NBC News, Washington Post). (Mar 28)
todaysEvents.delta: Houthis fire first missile at Israel - new front

todaysEvents.summary: Iran 'waiting' for ground invasion as US moves additional troops to Middle East; Iran parliament speaker Ghalibaf: 'enemy openly sends messages of dialogue and secretly plans a ground attack'; House Majority Leader Scalise confirmed conversations about ground options but 'no boots on ground today' (CNN, Al Jazeera). (Mar 29)
todaysEvents.delta: Ground invasion fears; US reinforces Middle East

todaysEvents.summary: Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia foreign ministers met in Islamabad on Mar 29 to seek de-escalation of the US-Israel war on Iran; Pakistan continues relaying US-Iran messages as sole active intermediary channel; Trump April 6 power plant deadline unchanged (Al Jazeera, CNN). (Mar 29)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Islamabad FMs summit (Mar 29: Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia) catalyses an interim framework: toll booth suspended, Iran accepts partial sovereignty language, Trump refrains from power plant strikes, Houthis halt Yemen-Israel operations. A 14-day ceasefire window opens. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl; JKM pulls back to $17-19/MMBtu on resumed Qatar damage assessment. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Tehran Toll Booth ($2M/vessel in yuan) becomes the new normal: some tankers pay to transit, others reroute via Cape. Houthis sustain Yemen-Israel missile strikes as a coordinated second front. Pakistan-hosted Islamabad talks begin but move slowly; Trump extends April 6 deadline again. Hormuz functions at reduced, tolled capacity - commercial P&I cover not restored. Brent settles $110-130/bbl; JKM at $19-23/MMBtu. Iran parliament legislates the toll, entrenching yuan-denominated petrotrade. iTraxx Asia IG pushes through 185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Trump resumes power plant strikes after April 6 upon Iran's toll booth legislation passing parliament; Houthis escalate to daily Israel strikes with precision weapons; ground invasion preparations confirmed. Iran revokes non-hostile framework, closes toll corridor; full commercial blockade reinstated. IRGC resumes full offensive operations against US/Saudi targets. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges; de-dollarization cascade accelerates as yuan-settled energy trade expands beyond Hormuz.
